briefing.title: Sell-Through vs. Inventory Size
briefing.detail: Purse Maison sold 42 units MTD (₱13.5M) from 1,592 listings — a 2.6% sell-through rate — while Miss Manila Luxe leads velocity with 75 units sold from only 486 listings (15.4% STR) and The Orange Box generates ₱35.9M on just 21 inferred sales at a ₱221K avg ticket.
briefing.action: Audit the 1,550 unsold listings for pricing competitiveness; start with the 28% 'Other' brand segment which may carry slower-moving non-hero models dragging overall STR.

briefing.title: Hermès Dominance & Birkin/Kelly Pipeline
briefing.detail: Hermès is 40.5% of Purse Maison inventory (≈645 units, ~₱115.9M estimated value) and accounts for 3 of the top 5 MTD sellers (Birkin, Kelly, Picotin); The Orange Box, the highest revenue competitor, also leans Hermès at 52.8%.
briefing.action: Protect Hermès restock priority — ensure Birkin and Kelly pipelines are replenished weekly, targeting at least 5–6 live Birkins and 8+ Kellys in combined sizes at all times.

briefing.title: Pricing Premium Risk on Hero Models
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's lowest asks on Birkin 25 (₱1.288M vs. market low ₱950K), Birkin 30 (₱770K vs. ₱575K), Kelly 25 (₱1.15M vs. ₱850K), Kelly 28 (₱735K vs. ₱480K), and Mini Kelly (₱1.54M vs. ₱970K) are all materially above market floor, creating stall risk on high-value SKUs.
briefing.action: Review condition, hardware, and year on the lowest-priced Kelly 28 and Birkin 30 units specifically — if Grade A/B, consider a ₱50K–₱80K price reduction to close the gap and accelerate turns on these two models first.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo Overhang
briefing.detail: Purse Maison carries 23 Chanel Double Flap Jumbos — the largest live count in the market (35 total across all stores) — with a lowest ask of ₱188K matching the market floor, suggesting margin is already compressed.
briefing.action: Pause new Jumbo acquisitions immediately and consider bundling or targeted promotions on 5–8 of the oldest Jumbo units to free up capital for faster-turning Hermès and Chanel Medium/Small Flaps.

briefing.title: Avg Days-to-Sell Competitive Position
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's 11.1-day average is slower than A&J Luxury (6.1d), Miss Manila Luxe (5.8d), Luxury Wish (6.5d), and The Bag Hub (7.7d), though these competitors operate at far lower avg tickets (₱33K–₱117K) which naturally accelerates volume.
briefing.action: Segment internal days-to-sell by price tier (under ₱200K, ₱200K–₱500K, above ₱500K) to identify whether slow turns are concentrated in a specific range and price accordingly.

briefing.title: Constance 24 Below-Market Opportunity Lost
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's Constance 24 lowest ask is ₱534.5K versus the market low of ₱395K — a ₱139.5K premium — while Constance 18 is priced at market floor (₱404K), suggesting inconsistent pricing strategy across Constance sizes.
briefing.action: Review the 7 live Constance 24 units: if any are standard hardware/Epsom in good condition, reprice 2–3 units to the ₱420K–₱460K range to stimulate movement and stay relevant to buyers comparison-shopping.

alerts: The Orange Box generated an inferred ₱35.9M MTD revenue on only 21 sales (₱1.71M avg sale value) — nearly 2.7x Purse Maison's ₱13.5M on double the units; they are winning the high-ticket Hermès buyer.

Mini Kelly (Kelly 20): Purse Maison at ₱1.54M exceeds market high of ₱1.68M on only 4 total market listings — extremely thin liquidity; verify these are not priced above realistic exit and flag for consignor negotiation if sourced externally.

Miss Manila Luxe sold 75 units at ₱77K avg ticket — their volume dominance in the sub-₱100K segment (73.4% 'Other' brand) signals strong mass-market demand Purse Maison is not capturing in the 'Other' 28.5% mix.

The Bag Hub shows a suspicious ₱678.8K total sold value on 14 units (₱48.5K avg per sale) despite a ₱116.5K avg ticket — possible reporting anomaly or deep discounting on LV and Bottega units; monitor for distressed inventory flood.

Zero aging90 inventory across ALL stores — either the tracking window is too early in July to surface 90-day items, or the market is clearing well; do not rely on this metric yet and revisit at month-end.

pricing.suggestion: Purse Maison floor at ₱1.288M sits ₱338K above market low (₱950K); maintain premium only on exotic/rare hardware units — for standard Togo/Epsom B25s, test repricing 1–2 units to ₱1.05M–₱1.1M to drive a sale and gather price elasticity data.

Floor at ₱770K is ₱195K above the ₱575K market low — if the ₱575K unit is a condition outlier, hold; otherwise reprice the weakest-condition B30 in inventory to ₱630K–₱650K to remain competitive and accelerate turns on this high-demand size.

With 11 live units and a floor at ₱735K versus the ₱480K market low, Purse Maison risks dead stock on mid-grade K28s; tier aggressively — price condition B/C units at ₱520K–₱580K and reserve the ₱735K+ ask for rare leathers or pristine condition A units only.

At 23 units and already at market floor ₱188K, avoid further price erosion — instead differentiate by condition grading, highlighting top-condition Jumbos at ₱250K–₱320K while quietly moving Grade B units via private offers at ₱188K–₱200K to loyal buyers.

Floor at ₱534.5K is ₱139.5K above market low (₱395K) with 7 units live; reprice 2–3 standard-hardware units to ₱430K–₱475K to capture mid-market buyers and reduce risk of all 7 aging into slow movers.

Purse Maison floor ₱280K is only ₱34.1K above market low ₱245.9K on 12 units — a defensible small premium; maintain current pricing but monitor the ₱245.9K competitor unit closely; if it sells within 7 days, hold; if it lingers, it signals price resistance and you can hold your floor safely.
